title: Reviewer identity must be independently bound before adoption
summary: A review approval and canonical-change proposal are insufficient for adoption unless reviewer identity evidence is independently verified, time-bounded, bound to the exact authorization and proposal digests, bound to the prior canonical queue blob, and accepted by a deny-by-default policy over provider, reviewer, verifier identity, and verifier artifact. Even valid identity evidence does not permit adoption; manual application and post-application verification remain mandatory. Six deterministic Node tests passed against byte-identical pre-commit files.

status_relations.observed: The committed evaluator creates a digest-bound reviewer identity binding only when provider signature verification is recorded, the authentication event is interactive human review, evidence is current, all authorization and proposal digests match, and a deny-by-default allowlist accepts the reviewer, provider, verifier, and verifier artifact.
status_relations.inferred: Reviewer identity is a separate authority domain from review content; binding both prevents an approved payload from being adopted under a substituted, stale, or self-asserted identity.
status_relations.proposed: Add a post-application verifier that compares the approved proposal, authenticated reviewer binding, prior canonical queue blob, manually applied commit, and resulting canonical queue blob.
status_relations.superseded: The absence of an executable reviewer-identity binding contract is superseded by the committed evaluator and adversarial tests.
status_relations.unresolved: No real identity-provider assertion, cryptographic verification artifact, authenticated reviewer event, manually applied queue commit, or post-application verification record currently exists.

falsification_or_review_route: Run node --test operations/continuity/reviewer-identity-evidence.test.mjs and reject this record if self-asserted identity, an unlisted reviewer, expired evidence, digest substitution, an untrusted verifier artifact, or automatic adoption is accepted. Structural validation alone must not be interpreted as cryptographic verification of a real provider assertion.
